title: Rounded Tender Jamaican Dumplings
description: Tender Jamaican dumplings made with plain flour and fermented to perfection, these dumplings are a show-off dish featuring a gluten-free friendly recipe.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the plain flour, cornstarch, salt, sugar, and active dry yeast.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Once the oil is hot, use a spoon to drop small balls of the dough into the oil, making sure not to overcrowd the pan.
Fry the dumplings for 2-3 minutes on each side until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the dumplings from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped scallions.
